segments.caption: The video begins with a screen recording of Logic Pro X, showing the interface with two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2' in the track list, alongside a Loop Browser on the right displaying various string and drum loops. The user interface is dark-themed, with visible transport controls and a tempo set to 120 BPM. The scene transitions to a stylized, dark, particle-filled animation featuring the 'DJ NEO' logo with the tagline 'LEARN ELECTRONIC MUSIC PRODUCTION ONLINE' and a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button at the bottom. This is followed by a bold black screen with red and white text reading 'LOGIC PRO INTRO CLASS #CLASS-1'. The video then returns to Logic Pro X, where a 'New Track' dialog box is open, showing options for 'Software Instrument', 'Audio', and 'Drummer'. The 'Software Instrument' tab is selected, with 'Empty Channel Strip' chosen as the instrument, and the 'Create' button is highlighted. The interface remains static, suggesting the user is about to create a new track. The overall atmosphere is instructional, with a focus on introducing Logic Pro X to beginners.

discovered_new_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing two software instrument tracks, a Loop Browser, and transport controls with a tempo of 120 BPM.
discovered_new_entities.New Track Dialog Box: A pop-up window in Logic Pro X with tabs for 'Software Instrument', 'Audio', and 'Drummer', currently set to 'Software Instrument' with 'Empty Channel Strip' selected and the 'Create' button highlighted.
discovered_new_entities.DJ NEO Logo Animation: A dark, particle-filled animation featuring a gold 'DJ NEO' logo with the tagline 'LEARN ELECTRONIC MUSIC PRODUCTION ONLINE' and a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button at the bottom.
discovered_new_entities.Intro Title Screen: A black screen with bold red and white text reading 'LOGIC PRO INTRO CLASS #CLASS-1', serving as a title card for the tutorial.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displaying a dark-themed digital audio workstation. A New Track Dialog Box is prominently open, set to 'Software Instrument' with 'Empty Channel Strip' selected. The user interacts with the dialog box, scrolling through the 'Instrument' dropdown menu to explore options like 'Alchemy', 'Drum Synth', 'ES1', and 'Vintage Electric Piano'. After reviewing the list, the user selects 'Empty Channel Strip' again and clicks the 'Create' button, which closes the dialog box and adds a new software instrument track labeled 'Inst 1' to the project. The interface now shows the newly created track with its channel strip, including controls for EQ, MIDI FX, and volume, while the main timeline remains empty, ready for recording or editing.

active_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing a newly created software instrument track labeled 'Inst 1', with a channel strip containing EQ, MIDI FX, and volume controls, and an empty timeline ready for use.
active_entities.New Track Dialog Box: A pop-up window in Logic Pro X with tabs for 'Software Instrument', 'Audio', and 'Drummer', currently set to 'Software Instrument' with 'Empty Channel Strip' selected and the 'Create' button clicked to finalize track creation.

segments.caption: The video begins with a view of the Logic Pro X Interface, showing a newly created software instrument track labeled 'Inst 1' with an empty timeline. The user interacts with the interface by adding a second track, 'Inst 2', resulting in two parallel software instrument tracks visible in the track list. The user then toggles the record enable button (red 'R') on both tracks, alternating between enabling and disabling recording for each. Subsequently, the user accesses the 'Logic Pro' menu from the top-left corner and selects 'About Logic Pro', revealing a pop-up window displaying version 10.6.1 along with copyright and technology credits. After closing the pop-up, the user clicks on the time display in the control bar, opening a dropdown menu with time display options such as 'Beats & Project', 'Beats & Time (Large)', 'Time', and 'Custom'. The user selects 'Beats & Time (Large)', changing the time display to show '01:00:00:00.00' in a larger font. The interface remains dark-themed throughout, with all actions focused on track management and display customization within the DAW.

active_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', with both tracks having their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The timeline remains empty, and the control bar displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format after user selection.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displayed in its default dark theme, showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', both with their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The user interacts with the control bar, opening a dropdown menu to change the time display format from 'Beats & Project (Large)' to 'Beats & Time (Large)', which updates the timeline display accordingly. The user then clicks the play button, causing the playhead to move across the empty timeline, indicating playback. After stopping playback, the user navigates to the mixer view by clicking the 'Mix' button, revealing the channel strips for 'Inst 1', 'Inst 2', 'Stereo Out', and 'Master', each with faders, pan controls, and automation settings. The user briefly hovers over the 'Inst 1' channel strip before returning to the Tracks view. Throughout the segment, the interface remains static with no audio or MIDI regions added, and the control bar consistently displays the time signature as 4/4 and tempo as 120 BPM. The top menu bar shows the application name 'Logic Pro' and the project title 'Untitled - Tracks', with the system time indicating 'Sun 14 Feb 19:59' to '20:00'.

active_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', with both tracks having their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The timeline remains empty, and the control bar displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format after user selection.

segments.caption: The video begins with the user interacting with the Logic Pro X Interface, specifically navigating the 'Window' menu to select 'Open Mixer'. This action opens the Mixer window, which displays a vertical arrangement of channel strips for 'Inst 1', 'Inst 2', 'Stereo Out', and 'Master', each with faders, pan controls, and mute/solo buttons. The user then closes the Mixer window and returns to the main Tracks view. Next, the user clicks on the 'Inst 1' track header, which triggers the Piano Roll editor to appear at the bottom of the screen. The Piano Roll is initially empty, showing a grid with piano keys on the vertical axis and time on the horizontal axis. The user hovers over the Piano Roll, and the interface displays editing options such as 'Time Quantize', 'Strength', 'Swing', 'Scale Quantize', and 'Velocity' in the left-hand panel. The user does not input any notes but explores the interface, with the cursor moving over the Piano Roll grid and the track headers. The timeline remains empty throughout, and the control bar at the top continues to display time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format, indicating no recording or playback has occurred. The overall environment is a dark-themed DAW interface with clear, functional layout elements.

active_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', with both tracks having their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The timeline remains empty, and the control bar displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format after user selection. The Piano Roll editor is opened for 'Inst 1', displaying an empty grid with piano keys and time divisions, along with editing parameters like Time Quantize, Strength, Swing, Scale Quantize, and Velocity in the left panel.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displayed, showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', both with their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The timeline remains empty, and the control bar at the top displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format after the user selects this option from the dropdown menu. The user then opens the Loop Browser, navigating through the 'Instrument' tab and scrolling through various loops such as '12 String Dream 01' and '12 String Dream 02', highlighting them to preview. The user scrolls further down the list, passing loops like 'Windy City Piano' and 'Wonderland Strings', before closing the Loop Browser. The user then opens the Note Pad under the 'Project' tab and types 'Project Tempo: 120 BPM', indicating the project's tempo setting. Throughout the segment, the interface remains dark-themed, and the user interacts with the software using mouse clicks and scrolling actions, focusing on setting up the project environment and exploring available loops.

active_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', with both tracks having their record enable buttons toggled on and off. discovered_new_entities.Loop Browser: A panel on the right side of the Logic Pro X interface that displays a list of available audio loops categorized by instrument, genre, and descriptors, with options to filter by scale, signature, and tempo. The user scrolls through loops like '12 String Dream 01' and 'Windy City Piano', highlighting them for preview.
discovered_new_entities.Note Pad: A text input area within the Logic Pro X interface under the 'Project' tab, where the user types 'Project Tempo: 120 BPM' to set the project's tempo. The Note Pad has a 'Done' button to save the input.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displayed, showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', both with their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The timeline remains empty, and the control bar displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format after user selection. The user navigates to the 'Project' tab and opens the Note Pad, typing 'Project Tempo: 120 BPM' to set the project's tempo. They then type 'Key Signature: C' to specify the key. Next, the user clicks on the 'Signature' dropdown in the control bar, revealing a list of key signatures, and selects 'E major'. The interface updates to reflect the new key signature. The user then clicks on the 'Tempo' dropdown, revealing options like '120', '6/8', '12/8', and 'Custom...', and selects '120' to confirm the tempo. The timeline now shows a blue tempo region labeled '120' spanning the entire visible area. The user then clicks on the 'Signature' dropdown again, selects 'A major', and the interface updates to reflect this new key signature. The Piano Roll editor is opened for 'Inst 1', displaying an empty grid with piano keys and time divisions, along with editing parameters like Time Quantize, Strength, Swing, Scale Quantize, and Velocity in the left panel.

active_entities.Note Pad: A text input area within the Logic Pro X interface under the 'Project' tab, where the user types 'Project Tempo: 120 BPM' to set the project's tempo and 'Key Signature: C' to specify the key. The Note Pad has a 'Done' button to save the input.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displaying two software instrument tracks, 'Inst 1' and 'Inst 2', both with record enable buttons toggled. The user interacts with the key signature dropdown, selecting 'D minor' before switching to 'C major'. The Loop Browser appears on the right, showing a list of loops categorized by instrument and genre, with the user scrolling through options like 'Irish Poly Harp 01' and 'Jack Impovy 01'. A 'DJ Neo subscribe to my channel' overlay with a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button appears briefly. The user then opens the Library panel, navigates to the 'Piano' category, and selects 'Yamaha Grand Piano', which loads into 'Inst 1' with a loading message. The track name updates to 'Yamaha Grand Piano', and the user switches to the Mixer view, revealing multiple channel strips with effects like 'Channel EQ' and 'Tape Delay'. The user adjusts the 'Bus 4' send on the 'Yamaha Grand Piano' track, and the Mixer displays various output and automation controls, including 'Stereo Out' and 'Bnce' faders, with the Master channel visible at the far right.

discovered_new_entities.Mixer View: The Mixer interface in Logic Pro X showing multiple channel strips with input, sends, output, and automation controls. The 'Yamaha Grand Piano' track is visible with effects like 'Channel EQ' and 'Tape Delay', and the user adjusts the 'Bus 4' send. The Master channel is shown at the far right with a 'Bnce' fader, and the view includes 'Stereo Out' and 'Read' indicators for each track.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X interface displaying two software instrument tracks, 'Yamaha Grand Piano' and 'Inst 2', both with their record enable buttons toggled. The user interacts with the 'Yamaha Grand Piano' track, adjusting its channel strip settings including MIDI FX and Audio FX, and then right-clicks to create a new MIDI region. A green MIDI region labeled 'Yamaha Grand Piano' appears on the timeline, which the user then extends to a length of 4 bars. The user double-clicks the region to open the Piano Roll editor, revealing an empty grid with piano keys and time divisions. In the Piano Roll, the user clicks to place three notes on the C3 key, which are then selected and dragged to extend their duration. The editing panel on the left shows parameters like Time Quantize set to 1/16 Note, Strength at 100, Swing at 0, Scale Quantize off, and Velocity at 80. The timeline displays the project in 4/4 time with a tempo of 120 BPM, and the control bar shows the time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format.

active_entities.Logic Pro X Interface: A dark-themed digital audio workstation interface showing two software instrument tracks labeled 'Yamaha Grand Piano' and 'Inst 2', with both tracks having their record enable buttons toggled on and off. The timeline initially remains empty, then a green MIDI region is created and extended to 4 bars. The control bar displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format after user selection. The Piano Roll editor is opened for 'Yamaha Grand Piano', displaying an empty grid with piano keys and time divisions, along with editing parameters like Time Quantize, Strength, Swing, Scale Quantize, and Velocity in the left panel.
active_entities.Mixer View: The Mixer interface in Logic Pro X showing multiple channel strips with input, sends, output, and automation controls. The Master channel is shown at the far right with a 'Bnce' fader, and the view includes 'Stereo Out' and 'Read' indicators for each track.

segments.caption: The video begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displaying a single 'Yamaha Grand Piano' track in the Piano Roll editor, where the user edits MIDI notes by dragging and adjusting their positions and velocities. The user selects and modifies individual notes, changing their timing and duration, and uses the Time Quantize settings to align them precisely. After editing, the user navigates to the track list and creates a new software instrument track named 'Inst 2'. The user then opens the 'New Track Dialog Box' to add a new instrument, selects 'Studio Strings' from the 'Software Instrument' options, and assigns it to 'Inst 2'. The Studio Strings plugin interface appears, showing controls for articulation, dynamics, and volume, with the user selecting 'Violins 1' as the articulation. The user then switches to the 'Library' panel, browsing through sound categories like 'Bass', 'Drum Kit', and 'Piano', before returning to the track view to continue editing. The timeline shows the 'Yamaha Grand Piano' region extended to 4 bars, and the control bar displays the time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format. The user interacts with the Mixer View, adjusting the 'Bus 4' send on the 'Yamaha Grand Piano' track, and the Master channel is visible with a 'Bnce' fader. The interface remains dark-themed throughout, with green MIDI regions and clear labels for tracks and parameters.

discovered_new_entities.Studio Strings Plugin Interface: A dark-themed plugin window for 'Studio Strings' with a violin graphic, featuring controls for Articulation (set to 'Violins 1'), Dynamics via CC, Last Played Articulation, Cutoff, Resonance, Volume, Attack, and Release. The interface includes navigation buttons, Copy, Paste, Undo, Redo, and a View percentage indicator.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with the Logic Pro X Interface displaying two software instrument tracks: 'Yamaha Grand Piano' and 'Inst 2', both with their record enable buttons toggled. The user creates a green MIDI region on the 'Yamaha Grand Piano' track, extending it to four bars. The Piano Roll editor is opened for this track, showing an empty grid with piano keys and time divisions, alongside editing parameters like Time Quantize, Strength, Swing, Scale Quantize, and Velocity. The user then copies the MIDI region to the 'Inst 2' track, which is renamed 'Strings' via a context menu. The Studio Strings Plugin Interface appears, showing controls for Articulation (set to 'Violins 1'), Dynamics via CC, Cutoff, Resonance, Volume, Attack, and Release, with a violin graphic. The user adjusts the volume fader for the 'Strings' track in the Mixer View, reducing it significantly. The control bar at the top displays time in 'Beats & Time (Large)' format, and the timeline cursor moves across the grid as the user navigates. The user right-clicks on the 'Strings' region, opens a context menu, and selects 'Rename Regions' to rename the region to 'Strings'. The track name in the left panel is also updated to 'Strings', and the user continues to adjust the volume fader, demonstrating the effect on the audio output.

segments.caption: The video begins with a view of the Logic Pro X Interface, showing two software instrument tracks: 'Yamaha Grand Piano' and 'Strings', both with their record enable buttons toggled on. The timeline displays two green MIDI regions, each spanning four bars, indicating that MIDI data has been recorded or placed. The control bar at the top shows the time signature as 4/4, tempo at 120 BPM, and key signature as C major. The user's cursor hovers over the interface, suggesting interaction, but no active editing occurs. The left panel shows the track mixer with volume faders and pan controls, and the 'Strings' track is selected, with its volume fader set to -11.2 dB. The interface remains static for the majority of the segment, with no visible changes to the MIDI regions or track settings. Towards the end, a red 'SUBSCRIBE' button appears at the bottom right, followed by a transition to a white screen with a red YouTube play button and the text 'DJ NEO' along with the tagline 'ELECTRONIC MUSIC PRODUCTION ONLINE' and a prompt to 'SUBSCRIBE FOR MORE VIDEOS LIKE THIS'. This marks the end of the tutorial segment and the beginning of the outro animation.
